articles.headline: Government Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing India as a global export hub for green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• Key objectives include setting ambitious production targets, developing a robust supply chain, and attracting significant domestic and foreign investment in the sector.
• The mission emphasizes R&D for advanced electrolyzer technologies, efficient storage solutions, and cost reduction strategies to enhance competitiveness.
• New incentives and subsidies for green hydrogen producers and consumers are expected, alongside a framework for green hydrogen certification and carbon credit mechanisms.
• This phase aims to create substantial green jobs, reduce reliance on fossil fuels, and contribute significantly to India's net-zero emissions targets by 2070.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Winter Session: Key Legislative Bills Under Intense Scrutiny
articles.explainer:
• The ongoing Winter Session of Parliament is witnessing vigorous debates on several crucial legislative bills, including proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act and the Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill.
• Opposition parties are pressing for detailed scrutiny of the Data Protection Bill, raising concerns over privacy safeguards and government access to data.
• Discussions are also underway regarding potential reforms to the criminal justice system, with a focus on modernizing procedural laws.
• Government aims to pass key economic reforms and social welfare legislations before the session concludes.
• The productivity of the session remains a key concern, with frequent adjournments over various contentious issues.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP) and enhancing bilateral defense and security cooperation.
• Both nations emphasized strengthening supply chain resilience, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors, to mitigate global disruptions.
• Discussions also covered cooperation in clean energy, digital transformation, infrastructure development, and joint R&D initiatives.
• India reiterated its support for Japan's vision for a rules-based international order and peaceful resolution of disputes in the region.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan in countering regional challenges and promoting economic stability.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Review: Repo Rate Maintained, Focus on Inflation Control Amid Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and core inflation, despite some moderation in global commodity prices.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC acknowledged the robust domestic demand and improving investment climate but flagged global economic uncertainties and geopolitical risks as potential headwinds.
• Future policy actions will remain data-driven, with a close watch on inflation trajectory, growth momentum, and global financial market stability.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Shamanuru Shivashankarappa, 84, Continues Active Public Service
articles.explainer:
• Shamanuru Shivashankarappa, a seasoned politician from Karnataka, continues his active political career at 84 years of age, serving as an MLA.
• He has a long and distinguished political career, having served as an MP from 1998–1999 and as a Karnataka MLA since 2004.
• Known for his contributions to education and social work, particularly in the Davangere region, where he founded several educational institutions.
• His continued presence highlights the enduring influence of experienced leaders in regional politics.
• His political journey reflects the changing dynamics of state-level politics in Karnataka over several decades.

articles.headline: ISRO Makes Significant Headway in Developing Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced substantial progress in the development of its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), designed for heavier payloads and future space missions.
• NGLV aims to be cost-effective, reusable, and capable of launching multiple satellites into various orbits, including Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO) and Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
• The design incorporates advanced propulsion systems, including semi-cryogenic engines, and modular architecture for versatility.
• This initiative is crucial for India's growing ambitions in space exploration, commercial satellite launches, and manned missions.
• NGLV is expected to replace or complement existing operational launchers like PSLV and GSLV, enhancing India's independent access to space.

articles.headline: E-Waste Management Rules 2025: Enforcement Commences, Focus on Producer Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has commenced the enforcement of the comprehensive E-Waste Management Rules 2025, replacing previous regulations.
• The new rules introduce stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets for manufacturers and importers of electronic goods, emphasizing recycling and proper disposal.
• A digital portal for tracking e-waste generation, collection, and recycling has been operationalized to enhance transparency and accountability.
• The rules also mandate awareness campaigns for consumers regarding responsible e-waste disposal and provide incentives for certified recyclers.
• This move aims to address the rapidly growing problem of e-waste in India, promoting a circular economy and mitigating environmental pollution.

articles.headline: India Progresses on AI Ethics and Governance Framework to Ensure Responsible Innovation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is finalizing a comprehensive framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance, aiming to balance innovation with responsible development.
• The framework focuses on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in AI systems, addressing concerns about bias and misuse.
• Consultations with industry stakeholders, academia, and civil society organizations have been integral to drafting the guidelines.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in ethical AI development, fostering an ecosystem that promotes trust and innovation.
• The framework is expected to provide clear guidelines for AI developers and deployers, particularly in sensitive sectors like healthcare, finance, and public services.

articles.headline: Coastal Erosion Mitigation Projects Underway in Vulnerable Regions of India
articles.explainer:
• Several coastal states, including Kerala, Maharashtra, and Odisha, have initiated large-scale projects to combat increasing coastal erosion, exacerbated by climate change and sea-level rise.
• Projects involve a mix of 'grey' infrastructure (seawalls, groynes) and 'green' infrastructure (mangrove restoration, artificial reefs, sand dune stabilization).
• The National Centre for Coastal Research (NCCR) is providing scientific and technical support, identifying critical erosion hotspots along India's coastline.
• Community participation and livelihood protection for coastal populations are integral components of these mitigation strategies.
• These efforts are crucial for protecting coastal ecosystems, infrastructure, and the livelihoods of millions dependent on coastal resources.

articles.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Projections Remain Optimistic Amid Strong Domestic Demand
articles.explainer:
• Leading economic forecasting agencies and the government maintain optimistic projections for India's Q3 (October-December) GDP growth, driven by robust domestic consumption and investment.
• The manufacturing and services sectors are showing healthy expansion, supported by festive season demand and government capital expenditure.
• While global headwinds persist, India's economic resilience is attributed to its strong internal market and ongoing structural reforms.
• Agricultural output is expected to contribute positively, contingent on favorable weather conditions for the Rabi crop.
• Challenges remain in managing inflation and ensuring equitable growth across all sectors and regions.

articles.headline: Discussions on Reforms in Judicial Appointments Process Gain Momentum
articles.explainer:
• Debates around reforming the judicial appointments process, particularly for higher judiciary, have gained renewed momentum in legal and political circles.
• Advocates for reform call for greater transparency, objective criteria, and a more diverse pool of candidates for judicial positions.
• The current Collegium system, while ensuring judicial independence, faces criticism for its opacity and lack of accountability.
• Suggestions range from establishing a National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) with modifications to refining the existing Collegium system.
• The goal is to ensure the appointment of meritorious and impartial judges, thereby strengthening the integrity and efficiency of the Indian judiciary.

articles.headline: UN Peacekeeping Reforms: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Overhaul and Greater Representation
articles.explainer:
• India, a leading contributor to UN Peacekeeping Missions, reiterated its long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms in the UN Peacekeeping architecture.
• Key demands include greater representation for troop-contributing countries (TCCs) in decision-making processes and enhanced mandates for peacekeepers.
• India also emphasized the need for better equipment, training, and technological upgrades for forces deployed in complex conflict zones.
• The discussions focused on improving the safety and security of peacekeepers and ensuring clear, achievable mandates aligned with ground realities.
• India's stance aligns with efforts to make UN Peacekeeping more effective, responsive, and representative of the global community.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands Reach to Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has intensified its efforts to expand its digital health services and infrastructure to remote and underserved rural areas across India.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for health records, allowing patients and healthcare providers to access medical histories digitally.
• Focus areas include generating Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs), digitizing patient records, and onboarding more healthcare facilities and professionals.
• Community health workers and local volunteers are being trained to facilitate digital health literacy and adoption in rural communities.
• This expansion is crucial for bridging the urban-rural healthcare divide, improving access to quality healthcare, and promoting health equity.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Ambitions Gain Traction with New Investment Proposals
articles.explainer:
• India's efforts to establish a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem are gaining momentum, with several new investment proposals under consideration.
• The government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for semiconductors and display manufacturing is attracting global players.
• Focus is on developing capabilities across the value chain, from chip design to fabrication and assembly, testing, and packaging (ATMP).
• This strategic push aims to reduce India's reliance on imports, enhance technological sovereignty, and create high-tech employment opportunities.
• Challenges include high capital investment, skilled workforce availability, and establishing a complete supply chain.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission: Progress Report Highlights Achievements in River Rejuvenation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Namami Gange Mission, showcasing significant achievements in cleaning and rejuvenating the Ganga river basin.
• Key successes include the operationalization of numerous sewage treatment plants (STPs), reduction in industrial pollution, and afforestation along the riverbanks.
• Emphasis has been placed on improving sewage infrastructure in towns along the Ganga and promoting decentralized wastewater management.
• The mission has also focused on biodiversity conservation, particularly for aquatic life like the Gangetic Dolphin, and public participation through 'Ganga Praharis'.
• Challenges persist in sustaining the momentum, dealing with non-point source pollution, and ensuring long-term ecological restoration of the river.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade Corridors

• Discussions focused on leveraging the Chabahar Port and the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) to improve access to Central Asian markets.

• India reaffirmed its commitment to providing humanitarian assistance and capacity-building support to the Central Asian nations.

articles.headline: Urban Flood Resilience Initiatives Launched in Major Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• Following recent heavy rainfall and urban flooding events, the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has launched new initiatives to enhance urban flood resilience in major Indian cities.
• The program focuses on integrated urban water management, including improving drainage infrastructure, restoring natural water bodies, and promoting permeable surfaces.
• Advanced early warning systems, real-time weather monitoring, and localized flood forecasting tools are being deployed.
• Community engagement and capacity building for disaster response at the local level are key components of the strategy.
• These initiatives aim to mitigate the impacts of climate change-induced extreme weather events and ensure sustainable urban development.

articles.headline: India's Indigenous Vaccine Development Program Shows Promising Results Against Emerging Pathogens
articles.explainer:
• India's robust indigenous vaccine development program has reported promising results in clinical trials for new vaccines targeting emerging infectious diseases.
• Research institutes and pharmaceutical companies are collaborating to develop vaccines against pathogens identified as high-priority threats by global health organizations.
• The focus is on leveraging advanced platforms like mRNA, viral vector, and protein subunit technologies to accelerate vaccine development and production.
• This initiative is critical for enhancing India's health security, reducing dependence on foreign manufacturers, and contributing to global health efforts.
• The program also emphasizes equitable access to vaccines, particularly for low and middle-income countries.

India's role as 'pharmacy of the world'.

Analyze the scientific and technological advancements driving rapid vaccine development, including new platforms like mRNA and viral vectors.

Suggest policy measures to foster a robust ecosystem for biotechnological research and innovation in India.

articles.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of vaccines globally, manufacturing 60% of the world's vaccines.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Implementation Report Highlights Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive report on the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, three years after its launch.
• The report highlights advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and establishment of multidisciplinary institutions.
• Significant strides have been made in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, as well as teacher training initiatives.
• Challenges include ensuring uniform implementation across diverse states, addressing digital divide in remote areas, and securing adequate funding.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands, fostering critical thinking, flexibility, and holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Year of launch of NEP 2020.
Key pillars of NEP 2020: Access, Equity, Quality, Affordability, Accountability.
Shift from 10+2 to 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary and holistic education.
Role of vocational education and skill development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative vision of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and its potential to reshape India's education landscape.
Discuss the progress made in implementing key reforms under NEP 2020, particularly in foundational literacy, vocational education, and higher education.
Evaluate the major challenges encountered in the nationwide implementation of NEP, including issues of funding, digital divide, and state-level variations.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to foster critical thinking, creativity, and holistic development among students.
Suggest strategies to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education for all.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in a learning activity, symbolizing educational reform.
articles.trivia: India's first National Education Policy was formulated in 1968, followed by one in 1986 (modified in 1992), before NEP 2020.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty, Address Technical Issues
articles.explainer:
• Technical experts from India and Pakistan held a constructive dialogue under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), addressing concerns regarding various hydropower projects.
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting focused on resolving differences over the design and operational aspects of certain Indian run-of-the-river projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT and seeking amicable solutions to water-related disputes.
• The dialogue is crucial for maintaining regional stability and ensuring equitable water sharing from the Indus river system.
• While significant political disputes persist, the IWT has largely endured as a successful example of water-sharing agreement.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Year of signing of Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).

Rivers allocated to India (Eastern Rivers): Ravi, Beas, Sutlej.
Rivers allocated to Pakistan (Western Rivers): Indus, Jhelum, Chenab.
Role of Permanent Indus Commission (PIC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance and enduring relevance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
Analyze the key provisions of the IWT regarding water sharing and the rights of both riparian states.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the IWT in recent decades, including technical disputes and geopolitical tensions.
Examine how the IWT has served as a model for transboundary water management despite broader bilateral conflicts.
Suggest mechanisms to further strengthen cooperation and dispute resolution under the treaty framework.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus river system and the allocation of rivers under the IWT.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah: Conservation Status Report Indicates Positive Adaptation for African Cheetahs
articles.explainer:
• A recent conservation status report on Project Cheetah indicates positive adaptation and breeding success among the African cheetahs translocated to Kuno National Park.
• The project, aimed at reintroducing cheetahs to India, has shown encouraging signs in terms of survival rates and territorial establishment.
• Wildlife experts highlight the importance of continuous monitoring, habitat management, and community engagement for the long-term success of the project.
• Challenges include managing predator-prey dynamics, mitigating human-wildlife conflict, and ensuring genetic diversity.
• Project Cheetah represents a significant step in India's wildlife conservation efforts and global big cat conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.

Last cheetah in India was declared extinct in 1952.
Difference between African Cheetah and Asiatic Cheetah.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance and conservation objectives of Project Cheetah in India.
Analyze the challenges involved in the reintroduction of a large carnivore like the cheetah, including habitat suitability and prey base management.
Evaluate the progress made by Project Cheetah in terms of cheetah adaptation, survival rates, and breeding success.
Examine the socio-economic implications of the project, particularly regarding local communities and human-wildlife conflict.
Suggest measures for ensuring the long-term viability and success of cheetah conservation in India.
articles.imageDescription: An African cheetah in the grasslands of Kuno National Park.
articles.trivia: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of running at speeds of up to 120 km/h over short distances.

articles.headline: India Champions Global South Cooperation on Climate Finance and Technology Transfer
articles.explainer:
• India continues to champion enhanced cooperation among Global South nations on critical issues of climate finance, technology transfer, and adaptation strategies.
• At a recent international forum, India advocated for developed countries to fulfill their climate finance commitments and facilitate easier access to green technologies for developing nations.
• Emphasis was placed on building collective resilience to climate change impacts and sharing best practices in sustainable development.
• India's leadership in the Global South is crucial for ensuring equitable and just climate action on the international stage.
• The discussions highlight the need for a unified voice from developing countries to address historical responsibilities and future climate challenges.

Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing adequate climate finance and advanced green technologies.
Evaluate the significance of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in international climate negotiations.
Examine the implications of climate change for Global South economies and the need for enhanced adaptation and loss and damage funding.
Suggest strategies for strengthening collective bargaining power of developing countries in global climate forums.
articles.imageDescription: A globe highlighting the countries typically considered part of the Global South.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' typically refers to countries in Africa, Latin America, and developing Asia.

articles.headline: Smart Farming Initiatives Boost Agricultural Productivity and Farmer Incomes
articles.explainer:
• Government and private sector collaborations are driving the adoption of smart farming initiatives, integrating technology to boost agricultural productivity and farmer incomes.
• These initiatives include precision agriculture using IoT sensors, AI-driven crop monitoring, drone-based spraying, and digital marketplaces for produce.
• Farmers are being trained on the use of modern tools and techniques, facilitating data-driven decision-making for optimal resource utilization.
• The aim is to make agriculture more sustainable, efficient, and resilient to climate change impacts.
• Challenges include initial investment costs, digital literacy among farmers, and reliable internet connectivity in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

prelimsPoints: Examples of smart farming technologies: IoT sensors, drones, AI, big data analytics.
Benefits of precision agriculture: optimized resource use (water, fertilizers), increased yield.
Government schemes promoting agricultural technology (e.g., e-NAM).
Challenges in adoption of technology in Indian agriculture.
Concept of 'Farmer Producer Organizations' (FPOs).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of smart farming technologies to revolutionize Indian agriculture, addressing challenges of productivity, sustainability, and farmer welfare.
Analyze the role of precision agriculture, IoT, and AI in optimizing resource utilization and mitigating climate change impacts on farming.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian farmers in adopting advanced agricultural technologies, including financial barriers and digital literacy.
Examine how government policies and private sector partnerships can accelerate the diffusion of smart farming practices.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to and benefits from agricultural technology for small and marginal farmers.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over a field, spraying crops, symbolizing smart farming.
articles.trivia: India is the second-largest producer of agricultural products worldwide.

highlights: Government unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting global export hub status and significant investment.
Parliamentary Winter Session witnesses intense debates on critical bills including Data Protection and Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act amendments.
India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concludes with focus on Indo-Pacific security, supply chain resilience, and economic cooperation.
RBI Monetary Policy Committee maintains repo rate, emphasizing inflation control amidst global economic volatility and robust domestic demand.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) expands its digital health services and infrastructure to remote rural areas, promoting health equity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production and export of green hydrogen.
2. It primarily focuses on the development of fuel cell electric vehicles.
3. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme is a key component of the mission.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global manufacturing and export hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is incorrect: While green hydrogen can be used in fuel cell vehicles, the mission's primary focus is broader, encompassing production, utilization in various sectors, and export. Statement 3 is correct: The SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers and producing green hydrogen, forming a core part of the mission.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) It indicates a policy of increasing liquidity in the financial system to boost economic growth.
(b) It implies a stance focused on gradually tightening monetary policy to control inflation, while ensuring growth remains supported.
(c) It refers to the RBI's decision to completely halt all lending to commercial banks.
(d) It suggests a policy of reducing the Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) to free up bank funds.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It implies a stance focused on gradually tightening monetary policy to control inflation, while ensuring growth remains supported.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance means the MPC is focused on pulling back the accommodative measures introduced during the pandemic to bring inflation back to the target level. It indicates a gradual tightening of monetary policy, not a complete halt or increase in liquidity, while still being mindful of supporting economic growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs regarding India's space program:
1. PSLV : Workhorse launch vehicle for LEO missions
2. GSLV : Capable of launching heavier satellites to GTO
3. NGLV : Designed to be cost-effective and reusable
Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correctly matched. PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is indeed known as ISRO's workhorse for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) missions. GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) is used for launching heavier satellites into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO). NGLV (Next-Generation Launch Vehicle) is being developed with a focus on being cost-effective, reusable, and capable of heavier payloads, replacing or complementing existing launchers.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), which of the following is/are its key objective(s)?
1. Creating Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) for citizens.
2. Providing free health insurance cover for all citizens.
3. Digitizing patient records and enabling interoperability across healthcare providers.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: A core component of ABDM is the creation of ABHA IDs for citizens to link their health records. Statement 2 is incorrect: Providing free health insurance cover is primarily the objective of Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY), not ABDM. ABDM focuses on digital health infrastructure. Statement 3 is correct: ABDM aims to create a seamless online platform for digital health records and ensure their interoperability across various healthcare facilities.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was mediated by which international organization?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, was mediated by the World Bank. It has successfully managed water sharing between the two countries despite periods of political tension.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the government's National Green Hydrogen Mission, particularly its Phase II objectives. Discuss the opportunities and challenges India faces in becoming a global leader in green hydrogen production and export. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its Phase II focus. Discuss opportunities: energy security, climate goals, economic growth, job creation, global market potential. Address challenges: high production costs, technology availability, infrastructure development, water availability, skilled workforce, international competition. Conclude with policy recommendations for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The debate around judicial appointments in India often oscillates between judicial independence and accountability. Examine the merits and demerits of the Collegium system and suggest reforms that could enhance transparency and public trust while upholding the judiciary's autonomy. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary; Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the Collegium system and its constitutional basis. Discuss its merits (judicial independence, expertise) and demerits (opacity, lack of accountability, potential for nepotism). Briefly mention the NJAC attempt and its fate. Suggest reforms such as a more diverse selection committee, clearer eligibility criteria, public disclosure of reasons for selection/rejection, and a consultative mechanism involving the executive, ensuring independence while addressing concerns of accountability and transparency.

mainsMocks.question: India's coastline is increasingly vulnerable to erosion and extreme weather events. Discuss the multi-pronged strategies, including both 'grey' and 'green' infrastructure, being adopted to enhance coastal resilience. What are the associated challenges and how can community participation be leveraged for sustainable coastal management? (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Important Geophysical phenomena; geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features. GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Define coastal erosion and its causes (natural and anthropogenic, including climate change). Explain 'grey' infrastructure (seawalls, groynes) and 'green' infrastructure (mangroves, sand dunes, artificial reefs) with examples of their application. Emphasize the role of community participation through awareness, livelihood integration, and local knowledge in sustainable coastal management.

quickBytes.text: Green Hydrogen: Produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, emitting zero greenhouse gases.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC): Six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR): A policy approach where producers bear significant responsibility for the post-consumer stage of their products.
Indo-Pacific: A biogeographic region of Earth's seas, increasingly a geopolitical concept for strategic cooperation.
ABHA ID: Ayushman Bharat Health Account ID, a 14-digit unique health identity for citizens under ABDM.
Collegium System: A system of appointment and transfer of judges that has evolved through Supreme Court judgments, not parliamentary law.
Namami Gange Mission: Flagship program for rejuvenation of River Ganga, launched in 2014.
Semi-cryogenic engine: Uses liquid oxygen and refined kerosene as propellants, more powerful than solid/liquid, less complex than cryogenic.
Chabahar Port: Located in Iran, strategically important for India to access Afghanistan and Central Asia, bypassing Pakistan.
Project Cheetah: India's initiative to reintroduce cheetahs, declared extinct in the country in 1952, from Africa.

GS1.headline: Coastal Erosion Mitigation Projects Underway in Vulnerable Regions of India
GS1.explainer:
• Several coastal states, including Kerala, Maharashtra, and Odisha, have initiated large-scale projects to combat increasing coastal erosion, exacerbated by climate change and sea-level rise.
• Projects involve a mix of 'grey' infrastructure (seawalls, groynes) and 'green' infrastructure (mangrove restoration, artificial reefs, sand dune stabilization).
• The National Centre for Coastal Research (NCCR) is providing scientific and technical support, identifying critical erosion hotspots along India's coastline.
• Community participation and livelihood protection for coastal populations are integral components of these mitigation strategies.
• These efforts are crucial for protecting coastal ecosystems, infrastructure, and the livelihoods of millions dependent on coastal resources.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

prelimsPoints: Causes of coastal erosion: natural (waves, currents) and anthropogenic (sand mining, dam construction).
Impact of climate change on coastal areas: sea-level rise, increased cyclone intensity.

Indian states most vulnerable to coastal erosion.

Examine the role of integrated coastal zone management (ICZM) in developing sustainable solutions for coastal protection.
Suggest policy interventions and community-based approaches to enhance coastal resilience.
GS1.imageDescription: A coastline showing signs of erosion with protective structures in the foreground.
GS1.trivia: India has a coastline of about 7,516.6 km, including the mainland and island territories.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Winter Session: Key Legislative Bills Under Intense Scrutiny
GS2.explainer:
• The ongoing Winter Session of Parliament is witnessing vigorous debates on several crucial legislative bills, including proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act and the Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill.
• Opposition parties are pressing for detailed scrutiny of the Data Protection Bill, raising concerns over privacy safeguards and government access to data.
• Discussions are also underway regarding potential reforms to the criminal justice system, with a focus on modernizing procedural laws.
• Government aims to pass key economic reforms and social welfare legislations before the session concludes.
• The productivity of the session remains a key concern, with frequent adjournments over various contentious issues.

Key provisions of the Data Protection Bill.

Evaluate the impact of the proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act on the cooperative movement in India.
Suggest measures to improve parliamentary productivity and the quality of legislative outcomes.
Examine the balance between national security and individual privacy in the context of data protection laws.

GS2.trivia: The first session of the Indian Parliament (Lok Sabha) commenced on 13 May 1952.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP) and enhancing bilateral defense and security cooperation.
• Both nations emphasized strengthening supply chain resilience, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors, to mitigate global disruptions.
• Discussions also covered cooperation in clean energy, digital transformation, infrastructure development, and joint R&D initiatives.
• India reiterated its support for Japan's vision for a rules-based international order and peaceful resolution of disputes in the region.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan in countering regional challenges and promoting economic stability.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Shamanuru Shivashankarappa, 84, Continues Active Public Service
GS2.explainer:

• His continued presence highlights the enduring influence of experienced leaders in regional politics.
• His political journey reflects the changing dynamics of state-level politics in Karnataka over several decades.

GS2.headline: Discussions on Reforms in Judicial Appointments Process Gain Momentum
GS2.explainer:
• Debates around reforming the judicial appointments process, particularly for higher judiciary, have gained renewed momentum in legal and political circles.
• Advocates for reform call for greater transparency, objective criteria, and a more diverse pool of candidates for judicial positions.
• The current Collegium system, while ensuring judicial independence, faces criticism for its opacity and lack of accountability.
• Suggestions range from establishing a National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) with modifications to refining the existing Collegium system.
• The goal is to ensure the appointment of meritorious and impartial judges, thereby strengthening the integrity and efficiency of the Indian judiciary.

GS2.explainer:

• Key demands include greater representation for troop-contributing countries (TCCs) in decision-making processes and enhanced mandates for peacekeepers.
• India also emphasized the need for better equipment, training, and technological upgrades for forces deployed in complex conflict zones.
• The discussions focused on improving the safety and security of peacekeepers and ensuring clear, achievable mandates aligned with ground realities.
• India's stance aligns with efforts to make UN Peacekeeping more effective, responsive, and representative of the global community.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands Reach to Remote Rural Areas

• Focus areas include generating Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs), digitizing patient records, and onboarding more healthcare facilities and professionals.
• Community health workers and local volunteers are being trained to facilitate digital health literacy and adoption in rural communities.

GS2.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Emphasizes Regional Connectivity and Trade Corridors

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Implementation Report Highlights Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive report on the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, three years after its launch.
• The report highlights advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and establishment of multidisciplinary institutions.
• Significant strides have been made in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, as well as teacher training initiatives.
• Challenges include ensuring uniform implementation across diverse states, addressing digital divide in remote areas, and securing adequate funding.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands, fostering critical thinking, flexibility, and holistic development.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty, Address Technical Issues
GS2.explainer:
• Technical experts from India and Pakistan held a constructive dialogue under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), addressing concerns regarding various hydropower projects.
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting focused on resolving differences over the design and operational aspects of certain Indian run-of-the-river projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT and seeking amicable solutions to water-related disputes.
• The dialogue is crucial for maintaining regional stability and ensuring equitable water sharing from the Indus river system.
• While significant political disputes persist, the IWT has largely endured as a successful example of water-sharing agreement.

GS2.headline: India Champions Global South Cooperation on Climate Finance and Technology Transfer
GS2.explainer:
• India continues to champion enhanced cooperation among Global South nations on critical issues of climate finance, technology transfer, and adaptation strategies.
• At a recent international forum, India advocated for developed countries to fulfill their climate finance commitments and facilitate easier access to green technologies for developing nations.
• Emphasis was placed on building collective resilience to climate change impacts and sharing best practices in sustainable development.
• India's leadership in the Global South is crucial for ensuring equitable and just climate action on the international stage.
• The discussions highlight the need for a unified voice from developing countries to address historical responsibilities and future climate challenges.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing India as a global export hub for green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• Key objectives include setting ambitious production targets, developing a robust supply chain, and attracting significant domestic and foreign investment in the sector.
• The mission emphasizes R&D for advanced electrolyzer technologies, efficient storage solutions, and cost reduction strategies to enhance competitiveness.
• New incentives and subsidies for green hydrogen producers and consumers are expected, alongside a framework for green hydrogen certification and carbon credit mechanisms.
• This phase aims to create substantial green jobs, reduce reliance on fossil fuels, and contribute significantly to India's net-zero emissions targets by 2070.

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Review: Repo Rate Maintained, Focus on Inflation Control Amid Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food and core inflation, despite some moderation in global commodity prices.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC acknowledged the robust domestic demand and improving investment climate but flagged global economic uncertainties and geopolitical risks as potential headwinds.
• Future policy actions will remain data-driven, with a close watch on inflation trajectory, growth momentum, and global financial market stability.

GS3.headline: ISRO Makes Significant Headway in Developing Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced substantial progress in the development of its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), designed for heavier payloads and future space missions.
• NGLV aims to be cost-effective, reusable, and capable of launching multiple satellites into various orbits, including Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO) and Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
• The design incorporates advanced propulsion systems, including semi-cryogenic engines, and modular architecture for versatility.
• This initiative is crucial for India's growing ambitions in space exploration, commercial satellite launches, and manned missions.
• NGLV is expected to replace or complement existing operational launchers like PSLV and GSLV, enhancing India's independent access to space.

GS3.headline: E-Waste Management Rules 2025: Enforcement Commences, Focus on Producer Responsibility
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has commenced the enforcement of the comprehensive E-Waste Management Rules 2025, replacing previous regulations.
• The new rules introduce stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets for manufacturers and importers of electronic goods, emphasizing recycling and proper disposal.
• A digital portal for tracking e-waste generation, collection, and recycling has been operationalized to enhance transparency and accountability.
• The rules also mandate awareness campaigns for consumers regarding responsible e-waste disposal and provide incentives for certified recyclers.
• This move aims to address the rapidly growing problem of e-waste in India, promoting a circular economy and mitigating environmental pollution.

GS3.headline: India Progresses on AI Ethics and Governance Framework to Ensure Responsible Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is finalizing a comprehensive framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance, aiming to balance innovation with responsible development.
• The framework focuses on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in AI systems, addressing concerns about bias and misuse.
• Consultations with industry stakeholders, academia, and civil society organizations have been integral to drafting the guidelines.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in ethical AI development, fostering an ecosystem that promotes trust and innovation.
• The framework is expected to provide clear guidelines for AI developers and deployers, particularly in sensitive sectors like healthcare, finance, and public services.

GS3.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Projections Remain Optimistic Amid Strong Domestic Demand
GS3.explainer:
• Leading economic forecasting agencies and the government maintain optimistic projections for India's Q3 (October-December) GDP growth, driven by robust domestic consumption and investment.
• The manufacturing and services sectors are showing healthy expansion, supported by festive season demand and government capital expenditure.
• While global headwinds persist, India's economic resilience is attributed to its strong internal market and ongoing structural reforms.
• Agricultural output is expected to contribute positively, contingent on favorable weather conditions for the Rabi crop.
• Challenges remain in managing inflation and ensuring equitable growth across all sectors and regions.

GS3.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Ambitions Gain Traction with New Investment Proposals
GS3.explainer:
• India's efforts to establish a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem are gaining momentum, with several new investment proposals under consideration.
• The government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for semiconductors and display manufacturing is attracting global players.
• Focus is on developing capabilities across the value chain, from chip design to fabrication and assembly, testing, and packaging (ATMP).
• This strategic push aims to reduce India's reliance on imports, enhance technological sovereignty, and create high-tech employment opportunities.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission: Progress Report Highlights Achievements in River Rejuvenation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Namami Gange Mission, showcasing significant achievements in cleaning and rejuvenating the Ganga river basin.
• Key successes include the operationalization of numerous sewage treatment plants (STPs), reduction in industrial pollution, and afforestation along the riverbanks.
• Emphasis has been placed on improving sewage infrastructure in towns along the Ganga and promoting decentralized wastewater management.
• The mission has also focused on biodiversity conservation, particularly for aquatic life like the Gangetic Dolphin, and public participation through 'Ganga Praharis'.
• Challenges persist in sustaining the momentum, dealing with non-point source pollution, and ensuring long-term ecological restoration of the river.

GS3.headline: Urban Flood Resilience Initiatives Launched in Major Indian Cities
GS3.explainer:
• Following recent heavy rainfall and urban flooding events, the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has launched new initiatives to enhance urban flood resilience in major Indian cities.
• The program focuses on integrated urban water management, including improving drainage infrastructure, restoring natural water bodies, and promoting permeable surfaces.
• Advanced early warning systems, real-time weather monitoring, and localized flood forecasting tools are being deployed.
• Community engagement and capacity building for disaster response at the local level are key components of the strategy.
• These initiatives aim to mitigate the impacts of climate change-induced extreme weather events and ensure sustainable urban development.

GS3.headline: India's Indigenous Vaccine Development Program Shows Promising Results Against Emerging Pathogens

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah: Conservation Status Report Indicates Positive Adaptation for African Cheetahs
GS3.explainer:

GS3.headline: Smart Farming Initiatives Boost Agricultural Productivity and Farmer Incomes
